cards.back: A stable state in a game where no player can improve their outcome by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.
A state in a game where no player can improve their outcome without making at least one other player worse off.
A strategy that is the best choice regardless of the actions of the other players in the game.
A strategy that involves choosing between different actions with different probabilities, often used in games where there is no dominant strategy.
A concept in game theory that combines the Nash Equilibrium with the idea of a perfect information game, where players know all the information about the game at each stage.
